info.name: StorageDevice
info.comment: Queries metadata for file system volumes.

description.p: The StorageDevice class returns metadata for mounted file system volumes. Set Volume before initialisation to identify the volume to query. If the volume name cannot be resolved, initialisation will fail.

After initialisation, the read-only fields describe the resolved volume. Values that depend on host or device support can remain unavailable; unknown byte counts are reported as -1, and DeviceID is empty if no identifier is available.

field.name: BytesFree
field.comment: Amount of storage space available to the current user, measured in bytes.

description.p: The available storage space reported by the host file system is indicated in this field. A value of -1 indicates that the amount could not be determined for the selected volume.

field.name: BytesUsed
field.comment: Amount of storage space in use, measured in bytes.

description.p: The amount of storage space used on the volume is indicated in this field. A value of 0 can indicate that usage could not be determined for the selected volume.

Please note that storage usage is typically measured in terms of blocks. For instance, a block size of 512 bytes will mean that this field is a multiple of 512. Two files of 1 byte each on such a file system would take up 1024 bytes of space and not 2.

field.name: DeviceFlags
field.comment: These read-only flags identify the type of device and its features.

field.name: DeviceID
field.comment: Unique device identifier for the mounted volume, when available.

description.p: If a volume exposes a unique device identifier such as a factory serial number, it will be readable from this field. The field is empty when the selected volume or file system driver does not provide an identifier.

field.name: DeviceSize
field.comment: Total storage capacity of the resolved volume, measured in bytes.

description.p: This field indicates the total byte capacity reported by the host file system for the selected volume. It does not reflect the remaining writable space; use BytesFree for that value. A value of -1 indicates that capacity could not be determined.

field.name: Volume
field.comment: The volume name of the device to query.

description.p: Set the Volume field prior to initialisation so that the object can query the selected volume. The standard volume string format is name:, but omitting the colon or defining complete file system paths when writing this field is also acceptable. Any characters following a colon are stripped automatically, and the stored value is normalised to include the trailing colon.

constants.const: This marker indicates that the presented volume is a bookmark and not a device name.
Catch-all for fixed media devices that are not directly identifiable.
Device is RAM, ROM, WORM, NVRAM, flashdisk or other form of memory. Does not guarantee a fast connection as it could be over a slow USB 1.1 connection for example
Device is a three dimensional printer.
Device media is removable from the hardware
Device media is removable from the hardware
Device is a two dimensional scanner.
Device is a three dimensional scanner.
Removable USB storage device. May be further qualified by HARD_DISK, FLOPPY_DISK etc
